calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jun-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Stephen Sayad, Esq. 415-410-1721 for Petitioner.
Kaitlyn Murphy, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-3867 for Respondent City and County of San Francisco.

RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER CARA DROGUS' Motion For Order Granting Petition For Writ Of Mandate.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Petitioner Cara L. Drogus's motion for an order granting verified petition for writ of mandate is granted for lack of jurisdiction.

It is undisputed that the incident that gave rise to the administrative decision took place at the East Beach Parking Lot in Crissy Field in the Presidio of San Francisco. The Presidio is a federal enclave in which, with limited exceptions not applicable here, the federal government has exclusive jurisdiction. (E.g., Standard Oil Co. of California v. California (1934) 291 U.S. 242, 243-244; United States v. Watkins (N.D. Cal. 1927) 22 F.2d 437, 438-441; see also Volk v. United States (N.D. Cal. 1999) 57 F.Supp.2d 888, 893 n.2 ["Aside from being widely recognized within the Northern District of California, this undisputed fact [that the Presidio is under federal jurisdiction] has been formally confirmed both legislatively and judicially."]; U.S. v. California Department of Transportation (N.D. Cal. 2009) 693 F.Supp.2d 1082, 1084 [noting that in 1994, jurisdiction over the Presidio was transferred from the United States Army to the United States National Park Service].) The City and County of San Francisco Department of Animal Care and Control therefore lacked jurisdiction to issue the decision and order challenged by the petition.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)Plaintiff may submit a proposed writ approved as to form.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Ruth Ekhaus, CSR#12219, rekhaus@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/EPS)
